docs.text: Resource Relevance "Scenario: Automated Shadow Monitoring Cutover Workflow" ⭐⭐⭐ "Advanced Scenarios & Test Coverage" ⭐⭐ "Shadow Monitoring SRE SOP" ⭐⭐ "da-tools CLI Reference" ⭐⭐ "Grafana Dashboard Guide" ⭐⭐ "Scenario: Same Alert, Different Semantics — Platform/NOC vs Tenant Dual-Perspective Notifications" ⭐⭐ "Scenario: Multi-Cluster Federation Architecture — Central Thresholds + Edge Metrics" ⭐⭐ "Scenario: Complete Tenant Lifecycle Management" ⭐⭐

docs.title: Scenario: Complete Tenant Lifecycle Management
docs.text: v2.1.0 | Related docs: getting-started/for-platform-engineers.md, getting-started/for-tenants.md, architecture-and-design.md §2.1

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This document describes the complete lifecycle of a tenant in the Dynamic Alerting platform, from launch to offboarding:

  • Preparation Phase (Pre-Launch): Assess requirements, plan alert strategy
  • Onboarding Phase: Establish configuration, deploy rules, verify alerts
  • Operation Phase: Daily monitoring, threshold tuning, mode management
  • Migration Phase: Migrate from legacy system (optional)
  • Offboarding Phase: Complete cleanup and archival

docs.title: Lifecycle Flowchart
docs.text:
graph TD
    A["New Tenant Request<br/>DBA submits"] -->|Evaluate| B["Preparation Phase<br/>- Database type<br/>- Exporter plan<br/>- Initial thresholds"]
    B -->|Approved| C["Choose onboarding path"]

    C -->|New system| D["Path 1: New Tenant"]
    C -->|Legacy system| E["Path 2: Migrating Tenant"]

    D -->|onboard| F["Onboarding Phase<br/>- scaffold_tenant.py<br/>- Config generation<br/>- Rule Pack deploy"]
    E -->|migrate| G["Migration Phase<br/>- onboard_platform.py<br/>- migrate_rule.py<br/>- shadow monitoring"]

    F -->|Verify| H["Verify launch<br/>diagnose + check-alert"]
    G -->|Verify| H

    H -->|Pass| I["Operation Phase<br/>- Daily monitoring<br/>- Threshold tuning<br/>- baseline_discovery"]

    I -->|Daily ops| J["Operation State Machine<br/>Normal / Silent / Maintenance"]

    J -->|Migration needed| K["Second migration<br/>If rule conversion<br/>or new exporter"]

    J -->|Offboarding request| L["Offboarding Phase<br/>- deprecate_rule.py<br/>- offboard_tenant.py<br/>- Config cleanup"]

    K -->|Migration complete| J
    L -->|Complete| M["Archive<br/>Retain audit logs"]

docs.title: Path 1: New Tenant Onboarding (No Migration)
docs.text: For tenants being set up from scratch (no legacy rule conversion needed).

docs.title: 1.1.1 Gather Requirements
docs.text:
# Confirm with tenant/DBA using form or ticket

□ Tenant name: e.g., db-product-01
□ Database type: PostgreSQL / MySQL / MongoDB / Redis / etc
□ Namespaces: ns-prod, ns-staging (can be multiple)
□ Cluster: Single cluster / Multi-cluster (multi-cluster: use "Multi-Cluster Federation" scenario)
□ Exporter status: Already exists / Need deployment
□ Initial threshold reference: Baseline data / Industry standard / Vendor recommendation
□ Notification recipients: DBA team email / Slack channel / Other
□ Priority: Tier-1 / Tier-2 / Tier-3 (affects alert severity and notification frequency)
□ Special needs: Silent mode planning / Maintenance windows / Custom annotations

docs.title: 1.1.2 Initialize Tenant Config Framework
docs.text:
# Use interactive mode
python3 scripts/tools/ops/scaffold_tenant.py

# Or non-interactive with parameters
python3 scripts/tools/ops/scaffold_tenant.py \
  --tenant db-product-01 \
  --db postgresql \
  --namespaces ns-prod,ns-staging \
  --non-interactive \
  --output conf.d/

# Output:
# - conf.d/db-product-01.yaml (tenant config framework)
# - scaffold-report.txt (planning document)

docs.title: 1.1.3 Plan Rule Packs and Exporters
docs.text: scaffold-report.txt includes recommended Rule Pack list. Select required Rule Packs based on DB type (see Rule Packs README), and confirm corresponding Exporters are deployed or in deployment plan.

docs.title: 1.1.4 Negotiate Initial Thresholds with DBA
docs.text:
Three strategies:

Strategy Approach Use Case A: Platform Defaults Use _defaults.yaml directly Quick launch, may need tuning initially B: Tenant-Specific Set values in conf.d/<tenant>.yaml Aligns with operations (recommended) C: Observe & Adjust Start with defaults, then da-tools baseline --tenant <t> --duration 604800 after 1 week Most conservative

docs.title: 1.2.1 Deploy and Verify
docs.text:
# 1. Verify config correctness
da-tools validate-config --config-dir conf.d/

# 2. Apply config to ConfigMap (see migration-guide §1 for injection methods)
kubectl create configmap threshold-config --from-file=conf.d/ -n monitoring --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

# 3. One-command comprehensive health check
da-tools diagnose db-product-01
# Expected: ✓ Configuration loaded, ✓ Metrics present, ✓ Alert rules loaded, ✓ Routing configured

Verification includes: threshold-exporter loaded, Prometheus metrics present, Rule Pack alert rules evaluating, Alertmanager routing configured.

docs.title: 1.2.2 One-Command Verification
docs.text:
# Comprehensive health check
python3 scripts/tools/ops/diagnose.py db-product-01 \
  --prometheus http://localhost:9090

# Expected output:
# ✓ Configuration loaded
# ✓ Metrics present: 50+
# ✓ Alert rules: 15+
# ✓ Operational mode: normal
# ✓ Notification channel: verified
# ✓ Health score: 95%

docs.title: Phase 1.3: Production Verification (Days 0–3)
docs.text:
# Monitor for 3 days to ensure no false positives or missed alerts

# Daily check
python3 scripts/tools/ops/diagnose.py db-product-01

# View live alerts
curl -s http://prometheus:9090/api/v1/alerts | \
  jq '.data.alerts[] | select(.labels.tenant == "db-product-01")'

# Verify notifications arrive
# - Confirm DBA received test notifications
# - Verify notification format is complete and actionable

docs.title: Path 2: Migrating Tenant (From Legacy System)
docs.text:
For tenants already running on other platforms needing migration to Dynamic Alerting. See scenario "Automated Shadow Monitoring Cutover Workflow".

Quick summary:

Phase Work Duration Prep onboard_platform.py (scan legacy rules) 2h Transform migrate_rule.py (generate new rules) 2h Validate validate_migration.py (7 days parallel test) 7d Cutover cutover_tenant.py (one-click switch) 30m

docs.title: 2.1.1 Daily Check
docs.text:
# Run every morning
python3 scripts/tools/ops/diagnose.py db-product-01
python3 scripts/tools/ops/check_alert.py MariaDBHighConnections db-product-01

# Checklist
□ operational_mode: normal / silent / maintenance
□ alert_count: Any abnormal increase or disappearance?
□ metric_lag: Any metric delay?
□ notification_backlog: Notification queue healthy?

docs.title: 2.1.2 Weekly Aggregation
docs.text:
# Generate multi-tenant health report weekly
python3 scripts/tools/ops/batch_diagnose.py \
  --output weekly-report.json

# Report includes:
# - Health scores for all tenants
# - Alert firing trends
# - Problem tenant list
# - Recommended adjustments

docs.title: Detect False Positives
docs.text:
# Observe historical patterns, get threshold suggestions
da-tools baseline --tenant db-product-01 --duration 604800
# Adjust thresholds in conf.d/<tenant>.yaml based on p95/p99 recommendations

docs.title: Detect Missed Alerts
docs.text:
# Confirm metric exists + rule evaluates + threshold set
da-tools diagnose db-product-01
da-tools check-alert PostgreSQLHighConnections db-product-01
# If threshold too high, lower and verify

docs.title: Backtest Threshold Changes
docs.text:
# Before modifying, backtest impact using historical data (compare new vs old config)
da-tools backtest --config-dir conf.d --baseline conf.d-old --lookback 7

docs.title: 2.3 Operational Mode Management
docs.text: Dynamic Alerting provides three-state operational modes for flexible alert control.

docs.title: 2.3.1 Normal Mode (Default)
docs.text:
# Example config
tenants:
  db-product-01:
    # No special config → normal mode
    # All rules evaluate and send notifications normally

Use case: Daily operations, all alerts active

docs.title: 2.3.2 Silent Mode
docs.text:
# Example: Disable all alert notifications (rules still evaluate, but notifications blocked)
tenants:
  db-product-01:
    _state_silent_mode:
      enabled: true
      expires: "2026-03-20T23:59:59Z"  # Optional; auto-recovers to normal after expiry
      reason: "Planned database maintenance"

Use cases:

  • Scheduled maintenance (backups, software upgrades)
  • Planned downtime (data migration, hardware repair)
  • Temporary incident triage (prevent alert storm)
  • Cost optimization (pause non-critical alerts)

Verification:

python3 scripts/tools/ops/diagnose.py db-product-01
# Output: operational_mode: silent
# Rules still evaluate, but alerts don't send notifications

docs.title: 2.3.3 Maintenance Mode
docs.text:
tenants:
  db-product-01:
    _state_maintenance:
      enabled: true
      recurring:
        - cron: "0 2 * * 0"    # Every Sunday 2 AM
          duration: "3600"      # 1 hour duration
          reason: "Weekly backup"
      expires: "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z"

maintenance_scheduler.py (deployed as CronJob) automatically creates silence rules in Alertmanager for each window. Deployment: da-tools maintenance-scheduler --config-dir conf.d/ --alertmanager http://alertmanager:9093.

docs.title: 3.1 Custom Rules
docs.text:
For tenant-specific monitoring needs not covered by default Rule Packs, deploy custom rules.

# 1. Write custom rules (Prometheus PromQL)
cat > custom-rules.yaml <<'EOF'
groups:
  - name: custom-db-product-01
    rules:
      - alert: CustomHighQueryTime
        expr: |
          histogram_quantile(0.95,
            sum by(le, tenant) (rate(query_duration_seconds_bucket{tenant="db-product-01"}[5m]))
          ) > 10
        for: 5m
        annotations:
          summary: "Query latency p95 > 10s on {{ $labels.tenant }}"
EOF

# 2. Validate custom rules
python3 scripts/tools/ops/lint_custom_rules.py custom-rules.yaml

# 3. Deploy
kubectl apply -f custom-rules.yaml

# 4. Verify loaded
curl -s http://prometheus:9090/api/v1/rules | \
  jq '.data.groups[] | select(.name | contains("custom-"))'

docs.title: 3.2 Multi-Namespace Tenants
docs.text:
For tenants running across multiple namespaces (prod-ns + staging-ns), specify in config:

# conf.d/db-product-01.yaml
tenants:
  db-product-01:
    _namespaces: ["prod-ns", "staging-ns", "canary-ns"]
    # threshold-exporter aggregates metrics from all namespaces
    # E.g., pg_connections aggregates connection counts across all namespaces

docs.title: 3.3 Cross-Cluster Tenants (Multi-Cluster Scenario)
docs.text:
For tenants with data across multiple K8s clusters, reference "Multi-Cluster Federation" scenario. Core principles:

  1. Each edge cluster deploys DB exporters
  2. Central collects metrics via federation or remote-write
  3. Central manages thresholds and alerting centrally
# conf.d/db-product-01.yaml (central)
tenants:
  db-product-01:
    _namespaces: ["prod-ns"]
    _cluster: "edge-asia-prod"  # Specify edge cluster location
    pg_connections: "70"
    _routing: { ... }

docs.title: 4.1 Prepare and Execute
docs.text:
# 1. Notify stakeholders (recommend 7-day advance notice)
# 2. Back up config
cp conf.d/db-product-01.yaml conf.d.archive/

# 3. Automated offboarding (recommended)
da-tools offboard db-product-01 --dry-run  # Pre-check
da-tools offboard db-product-01            # Execute

# 4. Offboard legacy custom rules (if migrated)
da-tools deprecate custom_pg_connections custom_pg_replication_lag --config-dir conf.d

docs.title: 4.2 Verify and Archive
docs.text:
# Confirm tenant fully removed
curl -s 'http://prometheus:9090/api/v1/query?query={tenant="db-product-01"}' | jq '.data.result | length'
# Expected: 0

# Archive backups
tar czf archive/db-product-01-offboarding-$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz conf.d.archive/

docs.title: Scenario A: Standard PostgreSQL Tenant (New Onboarding)
docs.text: Item Content Tenant name db-analytics Database PostgreSQL 13 Namespace analytics-prod Initial Rule Pack postgresql (required) + shared (required) Initial thresholds Use defaults Onboarding time 1–2 hours First adjustment Day 3–7 based on operational patterns

docs.title: Scenario B: Multi-Database Tenant (PostgreSQL + Redis)
docs.text: Item Content Tenant name db-cache-01 Database PostgreSQL + Redis (multi-exporter) Initial Rule Pack postgresql + redis + shared Exporter postgres_exporter + redis_exporter (dual deployment) Onboarding time 2–3 hours (dual exporter config)

docs.title: Scenario C: Cross-Cluster Tenant (Multi-Cluster Federation)
docs.text: Item Content Tenant name db-global Deployment 3 edge clusters (Asia + Europe + Americas) Config One in central, three edge relabel configs Rule Pack Complete set in central; each edge scrapes exporters Onboarding time 4–6 hours (3 edge clusters to configure) Reference "Multi-Cluster Federation" scenario

docs.title: Scenario D: Migrating Tenant (From Legacy System)
docs.text: Item Content Tenant name db-legacy Legacy system Nagios / Zabbix / Custom alerting Migration flow onboard → migrate → validate (7 days) → cutover Duration 7.5 days (validation dominates) Reference "Automated Shadow Monitoring Cutover Workflow" scenario

docs.title: Tool Quick Reference
docs.text: Tool Purpose Common command scaffold_tenant.py New tenant onboarding --tenant <name> --db <type> --output conf.d/ diagnose.py Health check <tenant> --prometheus <url> check_alert.py Alert status query <alertname> <tenant> baseline_discovery.py Threshold suggestions --tenant <name> --duration 604800 backtest_threshold.py Test threshold changes --tenant <name> --old-threshold 80 --new-threshold 75 batch_diagnose.py Multi-tenant report --output report.json lint_custom_rules.py Custom rule validation custom-rules.yaml offboard_tenant.py Tenant offboarding --tenant <name> --archival-dir ./archive/ deprecate_rule.py Rule offboarding rule-name-1 rule-name-2 --execute onboard_platform.py Pre-migration scan --legacy-config /path/ --output migration_input/ migrate_rule.py Rule migration transform --input hints.json --tenant <name> --output migration_output/ validate_migration.py Parallel migration test --mapping prefix-mapping.yaml --watch --auto-detect-convergence cutover_tenant.py Migration switch --tenant <name> --readiness-json file.json --dry-run

docs.title: Before New Tenant Onboarding
docs.text:
• Requirements complete (database type, namespaces, notification method)
  • scaffold_tenant.py output reviewed
  • Rule Pack selection confirmed
  • Initial thresholds negotiated (or decision made for defaults+Day 3 tuning)
  • Exporter deployment plan confirmed

docs.title: After New Tenant Onboarding
docs.text:
• validate_config.py passes
  • diagnose.py shows normal output (health score > 80%)
  • Metrics and alert rules verified
  • Alertmanager routing verified
  • DBA received test notification and confirmed

docs.title: Before Migrating Tenant Starts
docs.text:
• onboard_platform.py scan complete
  • migrate_rule.py transform complete
  • validate_migration.py running
  • Shadow rules deployed
  • Alertmanager shadow route configured

docs.title: Before Migrating Tenant Cutover
docs.text:
• 7 days zero-mismatch (or cutover-readiness.json auto-generated)
  • --dry-run preview confirms correctness
  • On-call SRE confirmed available
  • Rollback plan prepared

docs.title: Before Tenant Offboarding
docs.text:
• Stakeholders notified (7-day advance notice)
  • Config and data backed up
  • Historical data queried and saved
  • No other workflows depend on this tenant

docs.title: After Tenant Offboarding
docs.text:
• offboard_tenant.py executed
  • Archival complete (backups uploaded to long-term storage)
  • Cleanup verified (no orphaned config or rules)
